data.name: Placidus
data.description: Quadrant house system widely used in modern Western astrology. Houses are calculated from time-based divisions of the diurnal arc and depend strongly on birth time and location.
data.interpretation_note: Default modern Western house system. It can change planet-in-house interpretation significantly and needs a fallback for high-latitude charts.
data.runtime_usage: Use as the default natal chart house calculation method when birth time and location are precise and the latitude is compatible.

data.name: Whole Sign
data.description: Ancient house system where each house corresponds to one full zodiac sign, starting from the Ascendant sign.
data.interpretation_note: Robust sign-based system. It is less sensitive to the exact minute than quadrant systems, but still requires enough birth-time reliability to determine the Ascendant sign.
data.runtime_usage: Use for traditional readings, high-latitude fallback scenarios, or product modes where the chart should avoid distorted quadrant houses.

data.name: Equal House
data.description: House system where all houses are exactly 30 degrees, starting from the Ascendant degree.
data.interpretation_note: Keeps the exact Ascendant degree as the start of house 1 while avoiding unequal quadrant house sizes.
data.runtime_usage: Use when the engine needs 30-degree houses anchored to the exact Ascendant degree.

data.name: Porphyry
data.description: Quadrant house system dividing each quadrant between Ascendant, Midheaven, Descendant and Imum Coeli into three equal parts.
data.interpretation_note: Simpler quadrant-based alternative to Placidus. It can still move planets between houses compared with sign-based systems.
data.runtime_usage: Use as an alternative quadrant house method when the product or interpretation profile explicitly requests it.
